_metadata.description: Green certifications and eco-labels relevant to Tunisian entrepreneurs

certifications.name: ISO 14001
certifications.tunisia_body: INNORPI (Tunisian standards adoption), certified by international auditors
certifications.description: International standard providing a framework for organizations to protect the environment, respond to changing environmental conditions, and manage environmental aspects systematically.
certifications.cbam_relevance: Demonstrates environmental management for EU export compliance

certifications.name: ISO 50001
certifications.description: Framework for establishing, implementing, maintaining and improving an energy management system to enable systematic improvement of energy performance.
key_requirements: Conduct energy review and identify significant energy uses
Set energy performance indicators (EnPI) and baselines
certifications.cbam_relevance: Energy data tracking supports carbon reporting

certifications.name: ISO 14064
certifications.full_name: ISO 14064 — Greenhouse Gas Accounting and Verification
certifications.description: Three-part standard for quantifying, monitoring, reporting, and verifying greenhouse gas emissions and removals.
certifications.cbam_relevance: Directly supports CBAM carbon content reporting

certifications.name: ISO 14067
certifications.description: Standard for quantifying the carbon footprint of products using life cycle assessment methodology.
key_requirements: Quantify GHG emissions across product lifecycle
certifications.cbam_relevance: Product-level carbon data for CBAM declarations

certifications.name: Label Écologique Tunisien
certifications.issuing_body: ANPE (Agence Nationale de Protection de l'Environnement)
certifications.description: National eco-label program certifying products and services that meet Tunisian environmental standards. Less costly than international certifications, designed for local market.
key_requirements: Meet ANPE environmental criteria for the specific product/service category

certifications.name: Bio Tunisia (Organic)
certifications.issuing_body: Ministry of Agriculture / authorized certification bodies
certifications.description: Organic agriculture certification for Tunisian producers. Aligned with EU organic regulation for export eligibility.

certifications.name: OEKO-TEX Standard 100
certifications.description: Tests textiles for harmful substances at every stage of production. Essential for textile exports to EU.
key_requirements: Compliance with limit values for chemicals
benefits: EU market access requirement for many buyers
certifications.cbam_relevance: Not directly, but supports overall sustainability profile

certifications.name: Green Key
certifications.description: International eco-label for tourism facilities (hotels, camps, restaurants). Recognizes environmental commitment in the tourism sector.

certifications.name: GLOBALG.A.P.
certifications.description: Farm-level certification covering food safety, environmental sustainability, and worker welfare. Required by many EU retailers.
benefits: Required by major EU supermarket chains

cbam_info.name: EU CBAM — Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism
cbam_info.effective_date: Transitional phase started Oct 2023, full implementation 2026
cbam_info.description: EU importers must purchase CBAM certificates corresponding to the carbon price that would have been paid had the goods been produced under EU carbon pricing rules.
cbam_info.impact_on_tunisia: Tunisian exporters in affected sectors must provide verified emissions data to EU importers. Businesses not in these sectors may still face future CBAM expansion.
recommended_actions: Track and verify product-level carbon emissions
Consider ISO 14001 for overall environmental management
Engage with EU buyers on emissions data requirements
